acquisition method: MS/MS data on timsTOF Pro were acquired in the m/z range of 100–1700 and the 1/k0 range of 0.6–1.6 V × s × cm−2 using the DDA‐PASEF method
alkylation reagent: alkylation (55 mM IAA in 25 mM NaHCO3; 30 min, room temperature, 750 rpm)
alkylation concentration: alkylation (55 mM IAA in 25 mM NaHCO3; 30 min, room temperature, 750 rpm)
cleavage agent: incubated with 125 ng trypsin (Proteomics grade; Merc) in 50 mM NaHCO3
collision energy: HCD MS/MS spectra (isolation window m/z 1.2, 30% relative fragmentation energy)
enrichment method: followed by a Western blot using an antibody against the NdhH subunit
fractionation method: separation of protein complexes by Clear‐Native Polyacrylamide Gel Electrophoresis (CN‐PAGE)
instrument: connected to either Orbitrap Exploris 480 (Thermo Fisher Scientific) or timsTOF Pro (Bruker) spectrometers
ionization type: its emitter side was installed inside the CaptiveSpray ion source (Bruker)
labeling: MS analysis provided also crucial insights into stoichiometry of LHCA5
reduction reagent: protein reduction (10 mM DTT in 25 mM NaHCO3, 45 min, 56°C, 750 rpm)
reduction concentration: protein reduction (10 mM DTT in 25 mM NaHCO3, 45 min, 56°C, 750 rpm)
